params.description: 4 bags with 3 apples each appear. Full separation. Worked example designation visible.
Equation builder appears showing empty template: blank times blank.

beats.text: You've been matching expressions to equal groups. Now YOU get to build them, and you'll learn to go the other way too, figuring out what an expression looks like as a picture. Let me show you how building works.

elements.description: 4 bags with 3 apples each. Full separation. Worked example.
Equation builder showing empty template: blank times blank.

beats.text: First, I ask myself: how many GROUPS? Each bag is one group.

params.description: Bags highlight sequentially as guide counts.

beats.text: 1, 2, 3, 4. Four bags. That's 4 groups.

elements.description: 4 bags with 3 apples each. All bags highlighted after sequential count.
Equation builder showing empty template.

beats.text: Now I ask: how many in EACH group? Let me look inside one bag.

params.description: First bag focuses, apples inside highlight.

beats.text: 1, 2, 3. Three apples. Let me check another.

elements.description: First bag focused with 3 apples highlighted inside.
Equation builder showing empty template.

params.description: Second bag highlights.

beats.text: Also 3. They're equal groups — same number in every bag.

elements.description: Second bag highlighted showing 3 apples.
Equation builder showing empty template.

beats.text: So I have 4 groups of 3. Now watch how I build the expression.

params.label: groups
params.description: First slot glows with subtle label showing groups.

beats.text: The first number is how many groups. I click 4.

elements.description: 4 bags with 3 apples each visible.
Equation builder. First slot highlighted with groups label.

params.description: 4 appears in first slot. Guide demonstrates placement.

params.label: in each
params.description: Second slot glows with label showing in each.

beats.text: The second number is how many in each group. I click 3.

elements.description: 4 bags with 3 apples each visible.
Equation builder shows 4 in first slot. Second slot highlighted with in each label.

params.description: 3 appears in second slot. Complete expression shows 4 times 3.

beats.text: I built 4 times 3. This is called an EXPRESSION. An expression uses numbers and symbols to show the math. No equal sign, just the numbers and the times symbol.

elements.description: 4 bags with 3 apples each visible.
Equation builder shows complete expression: 4 times 3.

params.description: Equal groups visualization appears. 3 bags with 6 items each. Bags fully separated.
Equation builder appears below the equal groups. Two blank slots with times symbol between them.

beats.text: Your turn to build an expression. First question: how many groups do you see?
How many groups?

validator.description: Student selected 3, correct number of groups

beats.text: 3 groups.

elements.description: Equal groups showing 3 bags with 6 items each. Reading mode.
Equation builder with two blank slots and times symbol.

beats.text: Second question: how many in each group?
How many in each group?

validator.description: Student selected 6, correct items per group

beats.text: 6 in each.

elements.description: Equal groups showing 3 bags with 6 items each. Reading mode.
Equation builder with two blank slots and times symbol.

beats.text: Now build the expression.
Build the expression for the picture.

params.description: Equation builder fills with 3 times 6.

beats.text: You built 3 times 6. That expression shows 3 groups of 6.

elements.description: Equal groups showing 3 bags with 6 items each. Reading mode.
Equation builder displaying 3 × 6.

params.description: Left side: Equal groups visualization showing 2 bags with 5 items each. Concrete context: bags.
Right side: Equal groups visualization showing 2 boxes with 5 items each. Concrete context: boxes.

beats.text: Look at these two. On the left—bags. On the right—boxes.

elements.description: Left side: 2 bags with 5 items each in reading mode.
Right side: 2 boxes with 5 items each in reading mode.

params.description: Bags highlight briefly.
Boxes highlight briefly.

beats.text: Different containers. But notice what's the SAME.

elements.description: Left side: 2 bags with 5 items each.
Right side: 2 boxes with 5 items each.

params.description: Label '2 groups' appears on bags visualization.
Label '2 groups' appears on boxes visualization.

beats.text: Both show 2 groups.

elements.description: Left side: 2 bags with 5 items each. Label '2 groups' visible.
Right side: 2 boxes with 5 items each. Label '2 groups' visible.

params.description: Items in one bag highlight.
Items in one box highlight.

beats.text: Both have 5 in each group.

elements.description: Left side: 2 bags with 5 items each. Label '2 groups' visible.
Right side: 2 boxes with 5 items each. Label '2 groups' visible.

params.description: Expression '2 × 5' appears below bags visualization.
Expression '2 × 5' appears below boxes visualization.
Expression '2 × 5' highlights below bags.
Expression '2 × 5' highlights below boxes.

beats.text: Different containers—bags or boxes—but the SAME structure. Both are 2 groups of 5. Both build the same expression: 2 times 5.

elements.description: Left side: 2 bags with 5 items each. Label '2 groups' visible. Expression '2 × 5' below.
Right side: 2 boxes with 5 items each. Label '2 groups' visible. Expression '2 × 5' below.
Expression '2 × 5' displayed below bags visualization.
Expression '2 × 5' displayed below boxes visualization.

beats.text: Equal groups work the same way no matter what holds them.

elements.description: Left side: 2 bags with 5 items each. Label '2 groups' visible. Expression '2 × 5' below.
Right side: 2 boxes with 5 items each. Label '2 groups' visible. Expression '2 × 5' below.
Expression '2 × 5' displayed below bags visualization.
Expression '2 × 5' displayed below boxes visualization.

params.label: A
params.description: Option A: 3 bags with 4 items each. Equal groups configuration.

params.label: B
params.description: Option B: 3 bags with 4, 2, and 6 items respectively. Unequal groups configuration.

params.label: C
params.description: Option C: 4 bags with 3 items each. Equal groups configuration.

params.label: D
params.description: Option D: 4 bags with 3, 3, 1, and 5 items respectively. Unequal groups configuration.

beats.text: All of these show bags with items inside. But can I write a multiplication expression for all of them? Remember, multiplication shows EQUAL groups. Select the pictures where every bag has the SAME number inside.
Select ALL the pictures that show equal groups.

validator.description: Student selected A and C, both correct equal groups configurations

beats.text: A has 3 bags with 4 in each — equal groups, 3 times 4. C has 4 bags with 3 in each — equal groups, 4 times 3. B and D? Different amounts in the bags so they have unequal groups. We can't write a multiplication expression for those.

elements.description: Option A: 3 bags with 4 items each. Equal groups. Selected.
elements.label: A

elements.description: Option B: 3 bags with 4, 2, 6 items. Unequal groups. Not selected.
elements.label: B

elements.description: Option C: 4 bags with 3 items each. Equal groups. Selected.
elements.label: C

elements.description: Option D: 4 bags with 3, 3, 1, 5 items. Unequal groups. Not selected.
elements.label: D

params.description: Equal groups visualization appears. 5 boxes with 2 items each. Concrete context showing boxes containing items.
Equation builder appears with two blank slots and multiplication symbol. Tile palette showing tiles 1 through 10 visible below.

beats.text: You've learned that you need equal groups to write multiplication expressions and that equal groups work the same way no matter what holds them. These are important ideas for multiplication.

elements.description: Equal groups visualization. 5 boxes with 2 items each. Reading mode, full separation, boxes_items context.
Equation builder with two blank slots. Tile palette 1-10 visible.

beats.text: You've been choosing complete expressions to represent equal groups. Now you'll build them piece by piece using number tiles. Watch.

elements.description: Equal groups visualization. 5 boxes with 2 items each. Reading mode, full separation, boxes_items context.
Equation builder with two blank slots. Tile palette 1-10 visible.

params.description: Boxes highlight one by one in sequence.

beats.text: Notice all of the groups are equal. Same questions: How many groups?
5 boxes. That's 5 groups. I need the 5 tile in the FIRST slot.

params.description: Guide demonstrates dragging 5 tile from palette to first slot. Tile snaps into position.
First slot now filled with 5. Expression shows: 5 × [blank].

elements.description: Equal groups visualization. 5 boxes with 2 items each. Reading mode, full separation, boxes_items context.
Equation builder showing 5 × [blank]. First slot filled with 5, second slot empty. Tile palette 1-10 visible.

params.description: Items in one box highlight.

beats.text: How many in each group?
2 items. I need the 2 tile in the SECOND slot.

params.description: Guide demonstrates placing 2 tile into second slot. Tile snaps into position.
Complete expression now visible: 5 × 2. Both slots filled.

beats.text: I built it piece by piece—5 times 2. Now you try with tiles.

elements.description: Equal groups visualization. 5 boxes with 2 items each. Reading mode, full separation, boxes_items context.
Equation builder showing completed expression 5 × 2. Both slots filled. Tile palette 1-10 visible.

params.description: Equal groups visualization appears. 2 bags with 4 items each. Full separation.
Equation builder appears below. Empty slots with times symbol. Tile palette showing 1 through 10.

beats.text: Build the expression for these bags using tiles. First number: how many groups? Second number: how many in each group?
Build the expression.

validator.description: Student placed 2 in first slot and 4 in second slot, building 2 × 4

params.description: Equation builder confirms 2 × 4 with both tiles locked in place.

beats.text: You built it. 2 times 4 shows 2 groups of 4.

elements.description: Equal groups visualization showing 2 bags with 4 items each. Reading mode, full separation.
Equation builder displaying completed expression 2 × 4 with both slots filled. Tile palette visible.

params.description: Three boxes appear, each containing 5 items. Equal groups visualization in reading mode.
Equation builder appears below with two empty slots and times symbol. Tile palette shows 2, 3, 5, 8, 9, 12, 15.

beats.text: Build the expression. Same two questions: how many groups? How many in each?
Build the expression.

validator.description: Student placed 3 in first slot and 5 in second slot, forming 3 × 5

beats.text: Nice—3 times 5.

elements.description: Three boxes with 5 items each. Equal groups visualization in reading mode.
Equation builder showing completed expression 3 × 5 with both slots filled. Tile palette visible.

params.description: Equation builder appears showing completed expression 2 × 6 (no slots, static display).
Option A: 2 bags with 6 items each.
Option B: 6 bags with 2 items each.
Option C: 3 bags with 4 items each.

beats.text: We've been going from pictures to expressions. Let's flip it around. If I see 2 times 6, what does that MEAN?

elements.description: Equation builder displaying 2 × 6 (static, no slots).
Option A: 2 bags with 6 items each.
Option B: 6 bags with 2 items each.
Option C: 3 bags with 4 items each.

params.description: Option A containers flash subtly.

beats.text: The first number is groups. So 2 groups.

elements.description: Equation builder displaying 2 × 6.
Option A: 2 bags with 6 items each. Containers flashed.
Option B: 6 bags with 2 items each.
Option C: 3 bags with 4 items each.

params.description: Items in Option A highlight.

beats.text: The second number is in each group. So 6 in each.

elements.description: Equation builder displaying 2 × 6.
Option A: 2 bags with 6 items each. Items highlighted.
Option B: 6 bags with 2 items each.
Option C: 3 bags with 4 items each.

params.description: Option A highlights completely.

beats.text: The expression tells you what to look for. 2 times 6 MEANS 2 groups with 6 in each.

elements.description: Equation builder displaying 2 × 6.
Option A: 2 bags with 6 items each. Fully highlighted.
Option B: 6 bags with 2 items each.
Option C: 3 bags with 4 items each.

params.description: Equation builder displays expression 5 × 3 in static mode. No editable slots.
Option A: 5 bags with 3 items each.
Option B: 3 bags with 5 items each.
Option C: 8 items scattered with no equal group structure.

beats.text: Which picture matches 5 times 3?
Select the picture that shows 5 times 3.

validator.description: Student selected Option A, correct: 5 bags with 3 items each

params.description: Option A highlights to confirm selection.

beats.text: That's the one—5 groups with 3 in each.

elements.description: Equation builder displays static expression 5 × 3.
Option A: 5 bags with 3 items each, highlighted.
Option B: 3 bags with 5 items each.
Option C: 8 items scattered.

params.description: Equation builder appears in expression mode showing 4 × 5. Static display, no editable slots.
Empty equal groups workspace appears with build controls. Box icon visible. Container count and items-per-container both at 0.

beats.text: Now you build the picture. Show me: what does 4 times 5 mean?
Set the number of boxes. Then set how many in each box.
Add boxes and items to show 4 × 5.
4 boxes.

elements.description: Equation builder showing static expression 4 × 5 in expression mode.
Equal groups workspace showing 4 empty containers (boxes). Items-per-container at 0. Build controls active.

beats.text: How many items in each box?
You built it. All of the groups are equal. 4 boxes with 5 items in each. 4 groups of 5.

elements.description: Equation builder showing static expression 4 × 5 in expression mode.
Equal groups visualization showing 4 boxes with 5 items each, fully populated.

params.description: Equal groups visualization appears. 2 circles with 3 dots each. Semi-abstract context.
Equation builder appears with two empty slots and multiplication symbol. Tile palette displays numbers 1 through 10.

beats.text: Equal groups are not always in bags or boxes. Check out these circles. First question: how many groups do you see? Second question: how many in each group? Build the expression using those two numbers.
Build the expression.

validator.description: Student placed 2 in first slot and 3 in second slot, building 2 × 3

beats.text: 2 times 3. Same structure, different look.

elements.description: Equal groups visualization showing 2 circles with 3 dots each in reading mode. Semi-abstract context.
Equation builder displaying completed expression 2 × 3 with both slots filled. Tile palette shows 1–10.

params.description: Four circles appear, each containing 4 dots. Semi-abstract equal groups representation using circles as containers.
Equation builder appears with two empty slots and multiplication symbol. Tile palette below shows numbers 3, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, 10, 12, 14, 16.

beats.text: Build the expression. Remember the two questions: how many groups? How many in each?
Build the expression.

validator.description: Student placed 4 in groups slot and 4 in items slot, forming 4 × 4

beats.text: 4 times 4—both numbers are the same.

elements.description: Four circles, each containing 4 dots, in reading mode with full separation.
Equation builder showing completed expression 4 × 4 with both slots filled. Tile palette visible below.

beats.text: Last one. What does 5 times 6 look like?
Find 5 × 6.

params.description: Option A highlights to confirm selection.

beats.text: That's it. 5 groups, 6 in each.
